name: Profile_R5_ImagingSelection_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.ImagingSelection resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 ImagingSelection resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.ImagingSelection resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 ImagingSelection resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: R5: Date / Time when this imaging selection was created (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.issued` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.issued` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Selector of the instances (human or machine) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.performer` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.performer` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `ImagingSelection.basedOn` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.basedOn` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `ImagingSelection.category` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.category` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Imaging Selection purpose text or code (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.code` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.code` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
*All* code-value and, if present, component.code-component.value pairs need to be taken into account to correctly understand the meaning of the observation.

Element `ImagingSelection.studyUid` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.studyUid` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
See [DICOM PS3.3 C.7.2](http://dicom.nema.org/medical/dicom/current/output/chtml/part03/sect_C.7.2.html).

element.short: R5: The imaging study from which the imaging selection is derived (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.derivedFrom` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.derivedFrom` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The network service providing retrieval for the images referenced in the imaging selection (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Typical endpoint types include DICOM WADO-RS, which is used to retrieve DICOM instances in native or rendered (e.g., JPG, PNG) formats using a RESTful API; DICOM WADO-URI, which can similarly retrieve native or rendered instances, except using an HTTP query-based approach.

Element `ImagingSelection.seriesUid` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.seriesUid` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
See [DICOM PS3.3 C.7.3](http://dicom.nema.org/medical/dicom/current/output/chtml/part03/sect_C.7.3.html).

Element `ImagingSelection.seriesNumber` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.seriesNumber` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
See [DICOM PS3.3 C.7.3](http://dicom.nema.org/medical/dicom/current/output/chtml/part03/sect_C.7.3.html).

element.short: R5: The Frame of Reference UID for the selected images (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.frameOfReferenceUid` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.frameOfReferenceUid` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
See [DICOM PS3.3 C.7.4.1](http://dicom.nema.org/medical/dicom/current/output/chtml/part03/sect_C.7.4.html).

Element `ImagingSelection.bodySite` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.bodySite` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Related resource that is the focus for the imaging selection (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
An imaging selection may reference a DICOM resource that itself references other DICOM resources.
       e.g. a presentation state references a set of source images or frames.

Element `ImagingSelection.instance` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.instance` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `ImagingSelection.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Unknown does not represent "other" - one of the defined statuses must apply.  Unknown is used when the authoring system is not sure what the current status is.
